UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a player's core identity and meta-game data in the Axiom Frontier application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

username.description: The chosen display name for the player.

email.description: The public email address associated with the player's profile (not used for authentication).

creationDate.description: The date and time when the user profile was created.

lastLoginDate.description: The date and time of the player's last login.

currencyBalance.description: The current balance of in-game currency (e.g., Axiom Shards) held by the player.

experiencePoints.description: The total experience points accumulated by the player across all agents.

Agent.title: Agent
Agent.description: Represents a player's playable character or agent within the game world, encompassing its state and position.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Agent entity.

playerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity that owns this agent. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Agent)

name.description: The unique in-game name of the agent.

level.description: The current experience level of the agent.

health.description: The current health points of the agent.

energy.description: The current energy points of the agent, used for actions.

positionX.description: The current X-coordinate of the agent's position in the game world.

positionY.description: The current Y-coordinate of the agent's position in the game world.

positionZ.description: The current Z-coordinate of the agent's position in the game world.

directionX.description: The current X-component of the agent's facing direction.

directionY.description: The current Y-component of the agent's facing direction.

directionZ.description: The current Z-component of the agent's facing direction.

equippedInventoryItemIds.description: References to InventoryItem entities currently equipped by the agent. (Relationship: Agent N:N InventoryItem)

NPC.title: NPC
NPC.description: Defines a Non-Player Character (NPC) in the game world, including its personality and role.

id.description: Unique identifier for the NPC entity.

personalityDescription.description: An AI-generated description of the NPC's personality and characteristics.

role.description: The primary role or function of the NPC (e.g., 'Trader', 'Quest Giver', 'Enemy').

positionX.description: The current X-coordinate of the NPC's position in the game world.

positionY.description: The current Y-coordinate of the NPC's position in the game world.

positionZ.description: The current Z-coordinate of the NPC's position in the game world.

health.description: The current health points of the NPC.

questIds.description: References to Quest entities that this NPC is associated with or involved in. (Relationship: NPC N:N Quest)

WorldState.title: WorldState
WorldState.description: Stores global game parameters and dynamically calculated world indices, updated by the world heartbeat.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WorldState entity. Expected to be a singleton (e.g., 'global_world_state').

economicIndex.description: The current economic health index of the world.

militaryIndex.description: The current military strength index of the world.

stabilityIndex.description: The current political stability index of the world.

knowledgeIndex.description: The current level of technological and intellectual advancement.

culturalIndex.description: The current cultural vibrancy and influence index.

corruptionIndex.description: The current level of corruption impacting the world.

civilizationIndex.description: The calculated Civilization Index, derived from other world indices.

lastHeartbeat.description: The date and time of the last successful world heartbeat update.

inflationRate.description: The current global rate of economic inflation.

globalLootSaturation.description: A global modifier affecting loot generation based on environmental saturation.

Item.title: Item
Item.description: Defines the static properties of an item that can exist in the game world, inventory, or storefront.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Item definition.

name.description: The display name of the item.

description.description: A detailed description of the item's lore or functionality.

rarity.description: The rarity level of the item (e.g., 'Common', 'Rare', 'Epic', 'Legendary').

baseValue.description: The base monetary value of the item for trading or selling.

weight.description: The physical weight of the item, affecting inventory limits.

iconStoragePath.description: Firebase Storage path to the item's icon image for UI display.

modelStoragePath.description: Firebase Storage path to the item's 3D model asset for in-game rendering.

attackModifier.description: The attack bonus provided by the item if it is a weapon (0 if not applicable).

defenseModifier.description: The defense bonus provided by the item if it is an armor (0 if not applicable).

healingAmount.description: The amount of health restored by the item if it is a consumable (0 if not applicable).

magicFindModifier.description: A modifier affecting the chance of finding rare loot when this item is used or equipped.

isStackable.description: Indicates whether this item can be stacked in an inventory.

maxStackSize.description: The maximum number of items that can be stacked together if 'isStackable' is true.

InventoryItem.title: InventoryItem
InventoryItem.description: Represents a specific instance of an item held within an agent's inventory, including its quantity.

id.description: Unique identifier for this specific InventoryItem instance.

agentId.description: Reference to the Agent entity who currently owns this item instance. (Relationship: Agent 1:N InventoryItem)

itemId.description: Reference to the Item definition that this inventory item instance represents. (Relationship: Item 1:N InventoryItem)

quantity.description: The number of items in this stack.

Quest.title: Quest
Quest.description: Defines a quest available in the game, including its objectives and rewards, often generated dynamically by AI.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Quest definition.

description.description: An AI-generated detailed description of the quest, its narrative, and requirements.

objectives.description: An ordered list of strings describing the individual objectives to complete the quest.

rewardItemIds.description: References to Item entities awarded upon quest completion. (Relationship: Item N:N Quest)

rewardCurrencyAmount.description: The amount of in-game currency awarded upon quest completion.

experienceReward.description: The amount of experience points awarded upon quest completion.

startingNpcId.description: Optional reference to the NPC entity who initially gives this quest. (Relationship: NPC 1:N Quest)

associatedNpcIds.description: References to NPC entities that are involved in this quest (e.g., quest targets, follow-up NPCs). (Relationship: NPC N:N Quest)

PlayerQuestStatus.title: PlayerQuestStatus
PlayerQuestStatus.description: Tracks the specific progress and status of a quest for an individual player.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PlayerQuestStatus entity.

playerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity to whom this quest status belongs. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N PlayerQuestStatus)

questId.description: Reference to the Quest entity that this status pertains to. (Relationship: Quest 1:N PlayerQuestStatus)

status.description: The current status of the quest for the player (e.g., 'Accepted', 'InProgress', 'Completed', 'Failed').

currentObjectiveIndex.description: The index of the current objective the player is working on for this quest.

completionDate.description: The date and time when the quest was completed by the player (optional, only set upon completion).

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Records details of purchases and financial movements within the Axiom Storefront.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

playerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity who initiated the transaction. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Transaction)

transactionDate.description: The date and time when the transaction occurred.

status.description: The current status of the transaction (e.g., 'Pending', 'Completed', 'Failed').

paymentMethod.description: The method used for payment (e.g., 'PayPal', 'Axiom Shards').

amount.description: The monetary value of the transaction, in the specified currency.

currencyType.description: The type of currency used for the transaction (e.g., 'USD', 'AxiomShards').

purchasedInventoryItemIds.description: References to InventoryItem entities that were created or affected by this transaction. (Relationship: InventoryItem N:N Transaction)

AssetAssignment.title: AssetAssignment
AssetAssignment.description: Manages the dynamic assignment of assets (like textures) from Firebase Storage to 3D models in Godot at runtime.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AssetAssignment entity.

modelIdentifier.description: A unique identifier or path within Godot for the target 3D model or mesh instance (e.g., 'PlayerCharacter/MeshInstance3D', 'Sword_01').

assignmentType.description: The type of asset being assigned (e.g., 'Texture', 'Shader', 'Material').

materialSlotName.description: The name of the material slot on the model to apply the asset to (e.g., 'albedo_texture', 'normal_map').

assetStoragePath.description: The Firebase Storage path to the actual asset file (e.g., 'textures/player_skin_v2.png', 'models/sword_gltf.glb').

description.description: A brief description of what this asset assignment is for or which game element it affects.

definition.description: Stores a player's core identity and meta-game data. Each document is owned by the user matching the {userId} wildcard, ensuring private access. The 'id' field in the document MUST match the {userId} path parameter.

Represents a player's playable character. Each agent is owned by a specific user as indicated by the parent {userId} path. The 'playerId' field in the document MUST match the {userId} path parameter, ensuring Authorization Independence for rules.

Represents an item instance within a specific agent's inventory. Ownership is secured by the parent {userId} in the path. The 'agentId' field in the document MUST match the {agentId} path parameter. Authorization Independence is achieved as rules can verify `request.auth.uid == userId` without additional `get()` calls.

Tracks the status and progress of a quest for an individual player. Each status document is owned by the user matching the {userId} wildcard. The 'playerId' field in the document MUST match the {userId} path parameter, ensuring Authorization Independence for rules.

Records details of purchases and financial movements for a specific player. Each transaction document is owned by the user matching the {userId} wildcard. The 'playerId' field in the document MUST match the {userId} path parameter, ensuring Authorization Independence for rules.
Defines Non-Player Characters in the game world. These documents are publicly readable by game clients and writable only by authorized Cloud Functions or administrators.

Stores global game parameters and dynamically calculated world indices. This is expected to be a singleton collection (e.g., with document ID 'global_state'). Publicly readable, writable only by the scheduled 'world heartbeat' Cloud Function.

Defines the static properties of all items in the game. These documents are publicly readable by game clients (for item details) and writable only by authorized Cloud Functions or administrators (for game content updates).

Defines the static properties of quests. These can be AI-generated. Publicly readable by game clients (for quest details) and writable only by authorized Cloud Functions or administrators (for content generation/updates).

Manages dynamic asset assignments (e.g., textures) for Godot. These documents are publicly readable by Godot clients for runtime asset fetching and writable only by authorized Cloud Functions or administrators (for content updates).

A collection used for Database-Backed Access Control (DBAC) to define administrative users. The existence of a document at this path for a given {userId} indicates that user is an administrator. Following entityName derivation rules, it's 'UserProfile' as it refers to a user.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for Axiom Frontier is designed to be secure, scalable, and debuggable, adhering strictly to the provided core design principles and strategy mandates. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** This design primarily achieves Authorization Independence through the judicious use of path-based ownership and strategic denormalization. For all user-specific data (e.g., `Agent`, `InventoryItem`, `PlayerQuestStatus`, `Transaction`), the `userId` is embedded directly into the collection path (e.g., `/users/{userId}/agents/{agentId}`). This makes the owner's ID (`request.auth.uid`) directly accessible as a path parameter within security rules, eliminating the need for `get()` calls to parent documents to determine ownership. For instance, an `Agent` document intrinsically belongs to the `userId` in its path, and its security rules can simply check `request.auth.uid == userId`. Similarly, `InventoryItem` documents, nested under an `Agent`, also inherit this `userId` context from their path, making their rules robust without requiring cross-document reads.

**Clarity of Intent (Debuggability):** The hierarchical path structure for user data (`/users/{userId}/...`) explicitly states that data under a specific `userId` path belongs to that user. Global, publicly readable data like `NPCs`, `Items`, `Quests`, `WorldState`, and `AssetAssignments` are placed in top-level collections, clearly indicating their broad accessibility. 

**DBAC (No Custom Claims):** User roles, specifically for administrators, are managed via the `/roles_admin/{userId}` collection. The mere existence of a document in this collection for a given `userId` grants administrative privileges. This allows security rules to check for admin status using `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))` without relying on custom claims, making roles database-driven.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
1.  **Public Collections (`/npcs`, `/worldState`, `/items`, `/quests`, `/assetAssignments`):** These collections are designed for public read access. Security rules will simply allow `read` operations for all authenticated or unauthenticated users. This inherently supports `list` queries, as clients can retrieve any document without requiring server-side filtering. Clients are expected to query for specific documents or perform broad reads where all data is relevant.
2.  **Private User Collections (`/users/{userId}/agents`, `/users/{userId}/inventoryItems`, `/users/{userId}/playerQuestStatus`, `/users/{userId}/transactions`):** For these collections, the `list` operation is secure because the path itself contains the `userId`. Security rules will enforce `request.auth.uid == userId`. When a user queries `/users/{currentUserId}/agents`, Firestore's security rules automatically restrict the results to only the agents owned by `currentUserId`, without exposing other users' data. This ensures that `list` queries function correctly and securely, always returning a subset of documents that the requesting user is authorized to see.

**Invariants:** The structured paths (`/users/{userId}/...`) directly enforce ownership invariants. Timestamps (e.g., `creationDate`, `lastLoginDate`, `lastHeartbeat`) are part of the entity schemas, enabling rules to validate write operations based on these fields (e.g., ensuring `creationDate` is set only once). Denormalized fields, like `playerId` within `Agent` or the `userId` implicitly present in the path for `InventoryItem`, maintain data integrity for authorization purposes. 

**Structural Segregation:** All documents within a given collection path (`/users/{userId}/agents`, `/npcs`, `/items`, etc.) have a homogeneous security posture. Public entities are separated from private user-owned entities. This simplifies security rules, moving complexity from conditional logic into the data structure itself.
